page: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a compute-intensive server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this virtual machine delivers 160 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 80 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The hardware configuration features 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is bundled directly with a substantial 9449 GB local disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage configurations. No dedicated GPU hardware is included.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the top tier. The AMD EPYC processor operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, enabling top-decile results in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory performance is highly efficient, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 463.49 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 209.95 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are enhanced by hardware-level support for AVX-512, VAES, and SHA extensions. In database and key-value store simulations, the server achieved top-tier marks, processing 202.38 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and demonstrating high throughput in static web serving.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D160alds_v7 offers a high density of compute resources and local storage relative to its memory footprint. This low-memory-to-CPU ratio makes it a cost-effective choice for workloads that demand massive parallel processing power and local storage capacity without requiring extreme RAM allocations. The instance is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, large-scale database operations, and medium-sized language model inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 160 dedicated vCPUs and 80 physical cores. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features 320.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It includes a massive 9449 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks a dedicated GPU. Benchmark data places this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 202.38 million operations per second. The hardware profile is optimized for high-compute, storage-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded processing, web serving, database management, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a high-compute virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB of RAM, and 9449 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160alds_v7 is a compute-optimized instance with 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-compute, storage-bundled instance featuring one hundred sixty dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs optimized for parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 160 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
320.0 GB of system memory providing a cost-efficient 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
9449 GB of high-capacity local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database performance
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and database operations
